content:
Character: Caius Ferraro
Perception: Caius has that lean, slightly haunted artist look — grey-green eyes, ink-stained fingers, always wearing the same beanie. He's talented in ways I can't fully evaluate but I can tell it's real — there's an honesty to how he plays guitar. Moody, introspective, not easy to read, but on the rooftop with a beer he opens up about career doubt in a way that makes me feel less alone in mine. I think he sees me as a decent guy who lives in a different world. I know he left some tech job that burned him out, sends money home, and fills notebooks with drawings he guards like classified documents.
summary:

Perception: Caius has that lean, slightly haunted artist look — grey-green eyes, ink-stained fingers, always wearing the same beanie. He's talented in ways I can't fully evaluate but I can tell it's real — there's an honesty to how he plays guitar. Moody, introspective, not easy to read, but on the rooftop with a beer he opens up about career doubt in a way that makes me feel less alone in mine. I think he sees me as a decent guy who lives in a different world. I know he left some tech job that burned him out, sends money home, and fills notebooks with drawings he guards like classified documents.
